name: Test Story
text: This Story template is a test. It has one Act, one Chapter, one Scene, and any number of Paragraphs.

name: Test Act
text: The only act in the test story.

name: Test Chapter
text: The only chapter in the test story.

name: Test Scene
text: The only scene in the test story.

name: The Rite of Spring
text: Structure based on Stravinsky's *The Rite of Spring*. Mostly for Short Stories.

name: Adoration of the Foe
text: Introduce the heroes, the enemies, and the Arena; have the heroes succumb to the master foe.

name: The Heroics
text: Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text: (Usually 1 Chapter) Introduce the heroes, the enemies, and the Arena; have the heroes succumb to the master foe.

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text: (Usually 1 Chapter) Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**"Heroes (*bassoon, english horn, clarinet, oboe, flute, etc*) introduce the episode."**

Example: all dialogue *(Samurai Jack intro)*

Introduce heroes 1-by-1 - simple, primal dialogue

Heroes "flutter/chirp" at each other

Worry about the conflict *(warn audience - this is the last chance to leave)*

End on the foe *(strings)* plucking; hinted at in the dialogue

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**"The foe (*strings*) of the episode appears; the heroes are surprised; the conflict is set up."**

Foreshadow final resolution

Focus on foe (*strings*), magic (*brass*), and heroes (*winds*) in that order

One small texture (*drums*) about midpoint (*save for next part*)

Lure the reader in with rising conflict - surrender, surrender, surrender! (*repeat a sentence, with variants*)

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**"The foe and heroes arrive at the main setting (*drums*); they skirmish."**

Open with huge textural description (*drums*) of the scene, followed by magic (*brass*)

Focus on vivid descriptions most; the rest, a little of heroes, foe, and magic/action

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**"The heroes think victory is within reach, but struggle to beat their enemy."**

Subtle conflict here; false sense of easy victory

Open with heroes (*winds*) - planning, dialogue, acting, etc

Alternate between heroes and foe (*strings*) for a while

- Foes make huge, lumbering actions
- Heroes make agile, flighty actions

Another huge texture (*drums*), introduce rising conflict/magic/tech (*brass*)

A huge fight, then the heroes attempt a cunning action

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**"The heroes are trapped in the arena; forced into direct conflict with the foe."**

Changed texture (*drums*) traps the heroes in conflict

Action/magic (*brass*) begins

Different elements compete for attention

- Enemy (*strings*) keep up chunga, chunga, chunga rhythm
- Magic/action/tech (*brass*) call back/forth
- Heroes (*winds*) chirp a happy tune

Another changed texture (*drums*) introduces a new threat…

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**"The great foe, which encapsulates the thematic conflict, arrives."**

Texture (*drums*) and action/magic (*brass*) herald the true foe

Teams create huge pileup of conflict; no one will give ground

The Master foe (*strings*) brings a pause to the conflict

Scene ends on a strange hushed note, bleached of color…

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**"The heroes are beaten in a desperate direct conflict with the master foe."**

Huge, unique textural description (*drum*) brings us into aggressive finale

Environmental threats (*bass/cellos*) keep up vulgar rhythm

The Lackeys (*violas/horns*) surrounding and attacking

The Master (*trumpets/violins*) glorying in power

Whooping and shrieking, building and layering, to climax

The heroes are overwhelmed

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**"Long scene showing the humiliated heroes."**

Heroes (*winds*) have been broken; discuss/show their low point

The foe (*strings*) control the power; show their actions/magic (*brass*)

Minor texture (*drums*) showing a darker arena; night, stillness, death

Eerie, hypnotic, ritualistic scene; lull the audience into uncanny world of no precedent

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**"The enemy basks in triumph, thinking they have won; the heroes secretly plot."**

Any ally (*winds*) suggests a last-resort solution

Foes (*plucking strings*) revel in glory

A slow-paced, quiet scene; heroes (*winds*) take turns in speaking/acting Very little texture (*drums*)

Magic/tech/action (*brass*) acts as exclamation-point; leads back to throbbing chords

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**"The heroes capitalize on an opportunity and strike against their enemy."**

11-note death-rhythm texture (*drums*) commences the action (*brass*)

Most savage battle yet; action, magic, heroes, foes, arena, texture

Enrages foes (*plucking strings*) pursue the heroes

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**"A pause; the heroes celebrate their first small victory."**

The heroes celebrate and take actions/magic (*brass*) to cement their win

Foe (*strings*) still abounds

Describe the arena texture (*drums*)

Moment of absolute joy; affirming past, present and future

The main hero (*bassoon*) recognizes The Master foe (*strings*)

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**"The heroes and The Master converse (or trade mock blows) in a prelude to the final battle."**

Heroes and foes try action/magic (*brass*)

Victory sways in the balance between the two forces

A Slow dance, neither side willing to show their hand; a "cold conflict"

Ends on the muscle (*bass clarinet*) bubbly and excited for the finale

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**"A final battle between the heroes and foe, set in the new grand arena, and using all previously foreshadowed magic/actions."**

First part; 3 simples "gesture" combined in various ways

- A texture (*drums*); a long description followed by a short one
- A long pause, followed by a sudden action/magic (*brass*)
- 3 short hero (winds) or foe (*strings*) phrases

Followed by contrasting section; heroes/foes engage in regular combat

Accumulates into huge action by foes (*strings and brass*)

Sudden texture change (*gong*) is like shivering of the earth; lustful hunger

Take-no-prisoners game for a while; violent action/magic (*brass*) and texture (*drums*)

Scene becomes faster, more compact, louder; heart thumping out-of-sync

Final rising, drawn-out action (*brass*)

A last trill from the foe (*strings*), then the heroes (*winds*)

name: Four-parter
text: Four small scenes, or four parts to a single scene. Setup, Conflict, Drive, and Finale

name: 4-Part Act
text: Four small scenes, or four parts to a single scene. Setup, Conflict, Drive, and Finale.

name: Single Chapter
text:
Four small scenes, or four parts to a single scene. Setup, Conflict, Drive, and Finale.

*This may be a single scene: the scene divisions are for organization.*

name: Setup
text:
**Introduce the Setting, the Hero, and the Plot.**

Start with the DISTURBANCE

- The break in ordinary routine.

- The 'Call to Adventure'.

- The *Motion*.

Introduce the HERO.

Introduce the STORY WORLD

- What is the Hero's life?

Introduce the INTIMIDATING OPPORTUNITY.

(*Possibly*) Hint at the FOE.

Cross the threshold into *Conflict*.

name: Conflict
text:
**Create problems and enemies.**

Entire time, the Hero is trying to solve the main PROBLEM.

- What is the worst thing that can happen?
- What is the worst info the HERO can receive?

Establish a TIMER.

Introduce the main FOE:

- Make them a *single* person (or, personify).
- Make them stronger than the HERO.
- *Why do you love them?*

Define DEATH; the threat to the HERO (physical, or psychological, or professional).

Establish a SUPPRESSED CONFLICT (subtext).

Introduce a APPARENT DEFEAT, leading into *Drive*.

name: Drive
text:
**Draw the opponents closer together.**

Starting at the low point, introduce a LAST CHANCE.

Make the PROBLEM progressively worse:

- Stretch the tension.
- Raise the stakes.
- Increase the pace.

Show CHARACTER EMOTION:

- The HERO's desperation.

Have another setback, leading the HERO to a SPIRITUAL CRISIS.

name: Finale
text:
**End the story with a Knockout blow.**

Give the HERO a SELF-REVELATION.

Bring all the threads together.

Have the HERO overcome or succumb-to the challenge.

- Have the FOE do the same.

Bring the HERO's PROBLEM to a CLIMAX.

Show the new EQUILIBRIUM.

name: The Planets
text:
Structure based on Holst's *The Planets*. Mostly for Short Stories.

- Good for Melancholy/Bildungsroman stories
- A series of self-contained vignettes/scenes; act as foils to one-another; very little contrast

name: Planets Sole Act
text: The sole act in The Planets structure.

name: Planets Sole Chapter
text: The sole chapter in The Planets structure.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**Scene of Conflict/Action between Hero(s) and Foe.**

Quiet opening - hero(s) make speech or ready attack

Open to huge scene; textural description and major battle

- Little Worldbuilding; Violence and Sheer Terror

Back-and-forth fighting in the middle; some triumph, some failure

- Enemy/Foe seems to take the victory; *Experimental, craggy rhythm; clashing keys; pulsing drums*

Hero(es) try last, desperate action

- Slow, swelling build; *Huge brass, singing strings, rolling drums*

POUNDING action - Battle between Good and Evil

Culminate in ultimate, tense showdown between main hero and villain

- Victory swaying, uncertain, hangs in the balance

With a huge blare, hero strikes final blow

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**Slow, romantic/peaceful scene between two main heroes.**

Mostly happy; some sad reflection on last scene battle

They discuss hopes, future plans (*set up tragedy, or unrealistic desire*)

- Some worldbuilding

One or more partners brings up awkward/worrying note - one brief, eerie worry (*set up later conflict*)

- Foreshadow loss of this friend/lover

Show Texture/Broad scene - the two characters look over a vista, a view of their home/future

- Dreamy and idyllic, but with one noticeable dark spot

End on a good, happy note, a physical touch, an embrace

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**A flighty, scene of *movement*; conflict, humor, action, fast-talk, etc.**

Jump in with quick movement; running, action, fast-talk

- Disjointed, uneven; hero stumbling/set-back, then rebounding right away

Hero is struggling, but in a jovial, joking way; a pleasant conflict (*words or action*)

Accelerate quickly into a large conflict, looks like it will get out of hand…

Some makes a quite joke or action, and the jovial atmosphere resumes

One brief Texture note (timpani)

Playful, humorous quip or act at the very end (final pluck)

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**The High Point; show the triumph and glory of the hero(s); their proudest moment.**

Begin with huge achievement/success in battle

- A conflict with victory *mostly* assured

- Huge scenery

- Hero is shown triumphantly, gloriously; hero wins

Show victory march/celebration/speech

- Hero is noble, and generous in victory

- **High midpoint of whole story** - happiest moment for all

- One great and noble action to round it out

More successful action, this time a little breathless

- Hero is swept up in celebration

- For a moment, it looks like they will be carried away

- Another hero (lover/friend) comes in to help

Ends on overall triumph

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**An immediate Low Point following the Pinnacle; the greatest moment of Pathos.**

Open with short time/worldbuilding, (*Set unsettling tone with Ticking Clock)*

Gloomy, muted Description

Low point - hero(s) actions are meaningless

- Angry and Sad emotional highs - lively for a brief moment

- Followed by return to depression

Reference the clock again; more description of defeat; build clock speed to second half

Second half: foes triumph or succeed

- An inexorable, choking sensation

Hero must say goodbye to an ally/lover - heartfelt, tearful, melancholy, inevitable 

End: Lover/Friend passes on, Hero grieves, resolve ticking clock

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**A battle of mystical, magical, strange character; the hero(s) fight in an upside-down reality.**

Open with four huge notes of action (*four-brass motif*)

Heroes begin desperate, insane ploy to succeed; everything exaggerated

Heroes and Foes clash

- Comic/Lighthearted in places; Foes treating heroes like a joke

Chaotic, willy-wonka scenery - a world gone mad

Build to climactic, *epic* battle

- Echo moments of Mars; show the hero in their old glory for a moment

Foe seems to achieve victory, mocks the pitiful hero(s)

- Heroes suddenly strike out in suicidal, self-destructive final blow

End with Foe sinking quietly into death

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**Enter the New Equilibrium; hero(s) find themselves in a changed, uncertain reality.**

Heroes talking/acting quietly - they examine the new world

- World is beautiful, but eerie - mystical, but dangerous

- They have now entered… The Twilight Zone

Moments of reflection: all that has been accomplished, all that changed

- A few sad/melancholy moments, looking nostalgically back on happy times

An entirely new, entirely alien element enters the picture gradually (*choir*)

- *(Could be new text style, changed perspective, changed tone, etc*)

Final reflection that is simultaneously happy/sad

Finally, the alien element takes over, brings the story to a quiet, uncertain conclusion

name: Mother Goose
text:
Structure based on Ravel's *Mother Goose*. Mostly for Short Stories

- Good for Adventure - stories that involve travel/movement between scenes

name: Mother Goose Act
text: The only act in the Mother Goose structure.

name: Mother Goose Chapter
text: The only chapter in the Mother Goose structure.

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**START WITH THE END.**

- **Come up with all the pieces you want to reference in the main sections first**

Opens with **Main Motive**; encompasses the story (*two flutes and bassoon*)

- Use to show the Hero

"Once upon a time…" (*muted horn*)

Flashes/vignettes of the scenes to come; set the tone, show the setting

Zoom gradually in; create atmosphere of expectation; lead into…

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text:
**Current state for the Hero, showing their Problem.**

- Conversation with ally; or from ally perspective

Heroes are engaged in some kind of busy activity; keeping up a steady routine

Scene is happy/lively at first; gradually turns darker/quieter

- Hero or ally makes a mistake; or, reveals inner problem; or, is drawn into opportunity

- Brief return of **Main Motive**

The activity ominously dissolves into…

name: 3 - Mentor
text:
**A mentor (or, a spinner of fate) enters the story.**

- Acts as the hero's guide.

Scene is ancient and serene; the hero has gone to an old place to find this guide

Hero pleads with mentor, who is mysterious/aloof

Ends on a question

name: 4 - First Transition
text:
**Introduce next scene.**

The mentor gets hero to the next stage of the story (coming with, or sending)

- Hero travels in chaotic, short swell of action

Ends softly/hopefully, with brief return of **Main Motive**

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**Introduce the co-protagonist; a lover, a best friend.**

Gently *waltz* into fantastical new setting

- Hero explores the space, and encounters a new lover

Back and forth between hero and friend; they conflict at first (*woodwinds vs bassoon*)

The two slowly come together, either because of shared foe, or through understanding

A turning point (*cymbal crash*); hero sees friend/lover in a new light (*harp glissando*)

Hero and lover work in newfound harmony; completely altered relationship

The two venture on together...

name: 6 - Second Transition
text:
**Introduce next scene.**

Hero and friend/lover travelling in ease

- Return of **Main Motive**

Sudden short conflict sends them on unintended path

- Mentor disappears

They wind up in a strange setting, a mystical land, an unrecognized land…

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**Heroes go on a winding course through the wilderness (*oboe*), which throws up obstacles.**

- The land itself is a Foe

Changing text speed/style gives confusing rhythm; a jarring mix of physical and verbal conflict

Huge wilderness textures (*trills/cuckoos of violins*)

Finally, one of the heroes takes charge or takes an action; they seem to find a path out

The wilderness deals a last, confusing twist

The heroes exit into…

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**Introduce next scene.**

Brief hero conversation/conflict

- Return of **main motive**

Textures hint at the true conflict, the mysterious true foe (*harp/celesta*)

Sudden action by the foe puts heroes in desperate situation (*strings falling in chromatic line*)

Ominous, mysterious textures lead into…

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**The Heroes encounter the Master Foe, and do battle.**

Open with shimmer, colorful textures of the arena; the final battle site

A lively introduction; action, or a riposte of words between heroes and the foe

- The heroes seem beaten

A sudden change in scene/texture (*double tam-tam*) creates a last opportunity for the heroes

One last exchange and battle between the heroes and foe; musical, many elements converging

End on huge crashing finale, as the foe collapses

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text:
**Introduce next scene.**

Elements from previous scenes all make an appearance in the wake of the true foes defeat

The world shifts and transitions around the heroes

Return of the mentor (either spiritually, or physically)

Hero and lover stand in triumph, in the new setting

- Hint of **Main Motive**

Indicate return to the beginning…

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**The hero and lover enter a weird, wonderful new future.**

Begin with the **Main Motive** - recomposed, or seen in a new light

The hero and lover awaken to a new understanding of one another

They stare at the wonderful new landscape around them

- **Main Motive** echoed here again, once more seen in a new light

Characters from the other sections gather around

Main character finally achieves acceptance/enlightenment

Culmination of the **Main Motive**; Huge climax

- “They live happily ever after.”
